$comment: TF-C52 (D-LK-ARM64-EXTERN-DATA-ADDR-PIE-GOT): the address of an undefined extern FUNCTION taken as a LIVE value, then called INDIRECTLY through it. The arm64 RELOCATABLE .o materializes that address via the GOT (R_AARCH64_ADR_GOT_PAGE + LD64_GOT_LO12_NC) so a foreign default-PIE link accepts it -- witnessed structurally by test_elf_writer's GotExternAddrValueEmitsAdrGotPageAndLd64GotLo12OnAarch64 pin + the foreign gcc-PIE/qemu probe. THIS runnable example proves the SOURCE->binary->run chain on each shipped target: the taken address is the real `abs`, so g(1-argc-42) = abs(-42) = 42 -> exit 42. A `volatile` function-pointer blocks devirtualization + DCE so the `release` arm genuinely exercises the address materialization (not a masked no-op); `argc` (runtime) blocks const-folding. RED-ON-DISABLE: a wrong materialized address makes the indirect call land elsewhere -> not 42.
